item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
D I D
Y O U
J U S T

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
Noting that a recent series of tags were removed, as there were a few tags missing.

Readers, if you're not going to add all the tags for your article, it's best to just leave the page untagged and wait for an experienced tagger to take care of it. We have a team of people on the site who tag articles, and they search for pages that have no tags. Adding only a few tags prevents the team from finding the article, so if you're unfamiliar with the tags, it's totally fine to wait for them to take care of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not a terrible concept. Execution could use a lot of improvement, but not terrible. I'm pleased that you addressed the question of what happens if the culprit doesn't have any money.

'Trehiog' notion is dreadful, though. And if this thing only activates for insults, why would it go after swear words by themselves?

does a sincere "that's fucking brilliant!" activate it?

does "son of a bitch, i just stepped in dog shit" activate it?

and the test with Dr Martens is completely broken. First it punishes people for using insults, then it punishes someone for not using insults? I understand that you're trying to imply that it decided he's being passive-aggressive, but is there any way to satisfy this thing?

What about indirect insults? "You're much more intelligent than I expected from someone of your ethnic group! And I expected you'd smell terrible, but you don't!"

What about the types of ambiguous insults where there's a perfectly plausible alternate interpretation? e.g., the employer reference letter joke "you'll be very lucky if this man works for you" ?

edit: or statements of fact - "I don't like you" ? "You've repeatedly made serious mistakes, despite having lots of patient assistance, to the extent that I'm forced to question your basic competence" ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A bit rough around the edges, but it made me chuckle. Narrow +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Got it. I'll keep that in mind next time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Author, if you do not fully understand the tagging system, leave it for the tagging team. They target tagless articles, so an incompletely tagged article will be passed over.

item.title: Great!
item.content:encoded: This was a wonderful article to read. I love how the SCP works. Well written, with a good bit of dry humor in there. Using a scientist known to be rude to his colleagues as a test subject was a good touch. What I want to know, is where did the bloody jar come from?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty sure the second addendum should be Addendum 4280-B and not Addendum XXXX-B. Other then that, I like the concept and it seems well written to me.

I want this thing in real life. Just to uh… politely help some people. Yeah, that's it. trust me.

+1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
You krakeling suplan of a wereling trehiog!

Thanks to quantanaut, beertaster, Zyn, RockTeethMothEyes, not_a_seagull, and Gishface on IRC for helping me with the draft! I couldn't have done it without them!

Hope you guys enjoy!
